# rev17-change-manifest.md — canon.md rev 16 → rev 17

*Generated by the Integrator during the rev-17 pass. Companion to `canon.md` rev 17, `birds-eye-history.md`, and `vendor-facts.md`. Records every edit verbatim for the next Validator/audit pass.*

## Pass type & disposition

- **Structural decompression pass only.** The document was deflated by RELOCATING in-the-weeds content to external reference files and to the Closed ledger — nothing load-bearing was cut. **No new facts, no `<pages>` consumer-copy changes, no disposition reversals.**
- **Cardinal rule honored:** each relocation was written to its destination and verified lossless *before* the canon entry was tersed.
- **Disposition: ADOPTED.** All authority remains UNVERIFIED pending counsel + FMO; Locked decisions untouched.

Body-hash convention (locked this pass): the body is **lines 8→EOF** (everything below the L7 `---` divider, including the final newline). The `<pages>` block hash is **lines 452–1765 joined with `\n` plus a trailing newline** — note `<pages>` occurs as a substring six times in the file, so a naive first-occurrence index is wrong; only L452 is the block-opening tag.

## External relocation destinations

- **`birds-eye-history.md`** (14,676 B) — the full per-rev change-history narrative: a pre-rev-3 baseline section plus `## Rev 3` … `## Rev 16` sections. Generated verbatim-in-substance from the old 5.30 inline changelog. **Losslessness re-verified this pass:** reconstructing the inline 5.30 form from birds-eye sections reproduces the original byte-for-byte (13,014 code points; also archived at `530-original.txt`).
- **`vendor-facts.md`** (SHA-256 `39d8bfcb4e7066f974f15828f3723495038a18ea37287660837907d0b1ca2576`, 5,178 B) — Cloudflare Pages free-plan hosting limits, Cloudflare Customer DPA version/effective/supersedes dates + the rev-12 capture detail, and the Intulse A2P 10DLC registration mechanics (fees/locks, vetting limits, use-case menu, carrier-review/brand-match rules, sample-message rules). The binding legal posture stayed in canon; only operational detail moved. (Two items removed from a prior draft of this file this pass: a `~1–2 days` brand-verification timeline imported from the out-of-scope register row L1900, and an AgencyBloc-direct-POST restatement — neither was in the relocation source, and their real homes in canon are untouched.)

## Verbatim edits (old → new)

### [2a] partner-legal intro parenthetical — rev-16 L223

**OLD** (922 cp):
```text
Per-vendor operative legal sources for the website's data stack. Captured per a 2026-06-09 sourcing pass; treat every URL, version, and effective date as captured-as-of and UNVERIFIED — re-fetch and date-stamp at reliance (same posture as the regulatory authority table). Several entries are post-cutoff or version-sensitive (Cloudflare DPA v6.4 eff. 2026-04-03; AgencyBloc ToS eff. 2026-03-19) and were sourced by an external pass, not independently fetched here, so live URL re-verification rides with the partner-URL secondary pass (handoff doc). Whether a given DPA/BAA/term binds J&R's specific plan or contract — and whether MHMDA §060 (RCW 19.373) / NV NRS 603A.525 processor-binding requirements are met — is a counsel determination. Several vendor "HIPAA compliant"/"HITRUST" statements are marketing-page assertions; request and review the underlying SOC 2 reports, HITRUST certificates, and BAAs before relying.
```

**NEW** (968 cp):
```text
Per-vendor operative legal sources for the website's data stack. Captured per a 2026-06-09 sourcing pass; treat every URL, version, and effective date as captured-as-of and UNVERIFIED — re-fetch and date-stamp at reliance (same posture as the regulatory authority table). Several entries are post-cutoff or version-sensitive (e.g. the Cloudflare DPA, whose version/effective-date detail now lives in vendor-facts.md, and the AgencyBloc ToS) and were sourced by an external pass, not independently fetched here, so live URL re-verification rides with the partner-URL secondary pass (handoff doc). Whether a given DPA/BAA/term binds J&R's specific plan or contract — and whether MHMDA §060 (RCW 19.373) / NV NRS 603A.525 processor-binding requirements are met — is a counsel determination. Several vendor "HIPAA compliant"/"HITRUST" statements are marketing-page assertions; request and review the underlying SOC 2 reports, HITRUST certificates, and BAAs before relying.
```

### [2c] Cloudflare hosting cost/plan entry — rev-16 L259

**OLD** (700 cp):
```text
- Hosting cost/plan (Cloudflare docs captured 2026-06-12; re-fetch at reliance): the **Pages Free plan is sufficient for launch — Pro/Business NOT required**. Free-plan limits relevant here: 500 builds/month, 1 concurrent build, 20-min build timeout, 20,000 files/site, 25 MiB/file, 100 custom domains/project, unlimited bandwidth/requests, commercial use expressly allowed. All comfortably above a static Jekyll brochure site’s needs. Caveat carried at 5.2/5.5: Pages Functions and Workers bill against the SEPARATE Workers quota (100k req/day free) and a website-zone Pro upgrade does NOT lift Workers free limits — so adding any Function/Worker is both a cost event and a no-backend-posture break.
```

**NEW** (543 cp):
```text
- Hosting cost/plan (Cloudflare docs captured 2026-06-12; re-fetch at reliance): the **Pages Free plan is sufficient for launch — Pro/Business NOT required**. The free-plan build/file/domain limits (itemized in vendor-facts.md) are all comfortably above what a static Jekyll brochure site requires. Caveat carried at 5.2/5.5: Pages Functions and Workers bill against the SEPARATE Workers quota and a website-zone Pro upgrade does NOT lift Workers free limits — so adding any Function/Worker is both a cost event and a no-backend-posture break.
```

### [2d] Intulse 10DLC registration entry — rev-16 L249

**OLD** (2,096 cp):
```text
- 10DLC registration requirements (Intulse guide — <https://support.intulse.com/articles/10dlc-registration>; captured-as-of 2026-06-11, re-fetch at reliance): the guide's verbiage blocks are samples/templates (a "starting point" covering minimums), so adaptation is allowed; the binding constraints are the required opt-in element set (consent + brand name + use-case message types + frequency-varies + msg-and-data-rates + HELP + STOP + Privacy and Terms links) and that the opt-in carries the EXACT words of the registered use case. The privacy policy must state opt-in data is not shared with third parties for THEIR direct marketing, plus how to opt out; an opt-in checkbox is required anywhere the site collects a phone number; opt-in must be one-to-one, never implied, never buried in the Terms. Carrier review is manual against the live site; the brand name + EIN must match tax documents exactly and the brand info must match the website; one brand per EIN; support email/phone must be domain-based and shown on the Contact page. Fees/locks: $8 brand, $20 per campaign submission AND per rejected resubmission, $3–15/mo per campaign, 3-month TCR lock on submission even if denied; the use case CANNOT be changed once the campaign is created. Vetting is optional (unvetted limits — 2,000/day T-Mobile, 75 TPM AT&T — exceed J&R's volume). Use-case menu: Customer Care / Account Notification / Marketing are standard; Mixed and Low Volume Mixed take 2–5 sub-use-cases; Intulse-approved conversational UCaaS (Low/High) is a special use case barred from mass messaging. NEVER-allowed use cases include "3rd party lead generated services" (campaign-lead-path). Sample messages must match the use case, name the brand, include at least one with opt-out language, one per type if Mixed; embedded links must be declared and appear in a sample (no public URL shorteners); opt-in/opt-out/HELP confirmation texts are auto-populated for review; a verbal opt-in path is registrable with consent captured on a recorded line (call-recording-consent). Operational checklist: 10dlc-registration-readiness.
```

**NEW** (1,387 cp):
```text
- 10DLC registration requirements (Intulse guide — <https://support.intulse.com/articles/10dlc-registration>; captured-as-of 2026-06-11, re-fetch at reliance): the guide's verbiage blocks are samples/templates (a "starting point" covering minimums), so adaptation is allowed; the binding constraints are the required opt-in element set (consent + brand name + use-case message types + frequency-varies + msg-and-data-rates + HELP + STOP + Privacy and Terms links) and that the opt-in carries the EXACT words of the registered use case. The privacy policy must state opt-in data is not shared with third parties for THEIR direct marketing, plus how to opt out; an opt-in checkbox is required anywhere the site collects a phone number; opt-in must be one-to-one, never implied, never buried in the Terms. NEVER-allowed use cases include "3rd party lead generated services" (campaign-lead-path); a verbal opt-in path is registrable with consent captured on a recorded line (call-recording-consent). The procedural registration mechanics — the brand/campaign fees and 3-month TCR lock, the immutable-use-case rule, the optional-vetting throughput limits (T-Mobile/AT&T), the use-case menu taxonomy, the carrier manual-review and brand/EIN/website-match rules, and the sample-message content requirements — are itemized in vendor-facts.md. Operational checklist: 10dlc-registration-readiness.
```

### [4] Noted row 5.36 — rev-16 L1928

**OLD** (1,498 cp):
```text
- **5.36** Contact-form consent + a11y pattern LOCK (rev 16; decisions settled in build review 2026-06-12) — (a) two SEPARATE, unbundled checkboxes; (b) Box 1 (legal-doc clickwrap) is `required` and gates submit; (c) Box 2 (SMS opt-in) is NEVER `required` — optionality is a TCPA + CTIA/10DLC compliance requirement, not styling, and a `required` SMS box would contradict the visible "texting is optional" promise and break the consent’s validity; (d) accessible names via `aria-labelledby` pointing at the visible consent `<span>` — NOT `aria-label` (which would diverge the accessible name from the legal text it must mirror, WCAG 2.5.3 + a consent-integrity problem); (e) the SMS line carries the full element set (identified sender, message type, not-a-condition, frequency-varies, msg&data-rates, STOP, HELP, linked Privacy + Terms) and may keep its own links even though Box 1 also links them — the messaging consent’s own reachable disclosures are what 10DLC review expects; (f) the AgencyBloc honeypot field (`_Website`) stays OUT of the accessibility tree (`aria-hidden`) and tab order (`tabindex=-1`), hidden via the clip pattern not `display:none`, with the actual bot rejection enforced server-side on a non-empty value (a server-side read = the 5.2 trigger). The SMS opt-in WORDING itself remains DRAFT pending the sms-consent-scope reconciliation (Intulse/TCR language) — lock the pattern, not the final words. | clickwrap-assent, consent-record-capture, N11, sms-consent-scope, 5.2 |
```

**NEW** (1,426 cp):
```text
- **5.36** Contact-form consent + a11y pattern LOCK (rev 16; build review 2026-06-12) — (a) two SEPARATE, unbundled checkboxes; (b) Box 1 (legal-doc clickwrap) is `required` and gates submit; (c) Box 2 (SMS opt-in) is NEVER `required` — optionality is a TCPA + CTIA/10DLC requirement, not styling, and a `required` SMS box would contradict the visible "texting is optional" promise and break the consent’s validity; (d) accessible names via `aria-labelledby` pointing at the visible consent `<span>` — NOT `aria-label` (diverging the accessible name from the legal text it must mirror, WCAG 2.5.3 + consent integrity); (e) the SMS line carries the full element set (identified sender, message type, not-a-condition, frequency-varies, msg&data-rates, STOP, HELP, linked Privacy + Terms) and may keep its own links even though Box 1 also links them — the messaging consent’s own reachable disclosures are what 10DLC review expects; (f) the AgencyBloc honeypot field (`_Website`) stays OUT of the accessibility tree (`aria-hidden`) and tab order (`tabindex=-1`), hidden via the clip pattern not `display:none`, with bot rejection enforced server-side on a non-empty value (a server-side read = the 5.2 trigger). The SMS opt-in WORDING stays DRAFT pending the sms-consent-scope reconciliation (Intulse/TCR language) — lock the pattern, not the final words. | clickwrap-assent, consent-record-capture, N11, sms-consent-scope, 5.2 |
```
